title: Synthetic Paper A: Reproducible Behavior Cloning for Multi-Intent NPC Control
original_problem: imitate expert NPC trajectories in a fixed scenario distribution
method_summary: a recurrent policy maps ordered observations and intent tokens to a categorical action distribution
reusable_component: frozen recurrent observation encoder and categorical action head
input_semantics: ordered observation history plus explicit intent token
output_semantics: probability distribution over the complete discrete action vocabulary
compute_cost: 4.8 ms median decision latency in the synthetic fixture
limitations: expert data covers only familiar scenario combinations
the action head does not encode scenario-invalid actions

title: Synthetic Paper B: Semantic Invalid-Action Masking for Safe Policy Optimization
original_problem: prevent structurally invalid actions in discrete control
method_summary: a rule-derived binary mask removes invalid actions before categorical normalization
reusable_component: pre-normalization action-validity mask with explicit state-to-action semantics
input_semantics: current scenario state and discrete action ontology
output_semantics: binary validity indicator for every action in the shared action vocabulary
compute_cost: linear in the number of actions and below 0.5 ms in the fixture
limitations: cannot identify semantically poor actions that remain technically valid

title: Synthetic Paper C: Uncertainty-Gated Residual Policies under Distribution Shift
original_problem: adapt a frozen controller only in uncertain states
method_summary: a calibrated uncertainty score gates a low-capacity residual policy that corrects frozen policy logits
reusable_component: calibrated uncertainty gate and bounded residual-logit correction
input_semantics: frozen policy features, logits, and calibrated uncertainty
output_semantics: bounded correction over the same ordered action vocabulary
compute_cost: approximately 8 percent additional decision latency in the fixture
limitations: poor calibration may activate corrections too often
residual learning can overfit rare failure states

title: Synthetic Paper D: Shift-Robust Ensemble Policy for NPC Control
original_problem: improve NPC control under scenario distribution shift
method_summary: an ensemble of independently trained behavior policies averages calibrated action values under shift
reusable_component: full comparator implementation and fixed evaluation recipe
input_semantics: the same ordered observations and intent tokens as the baseline
output_semantics: calibrated action values over the same discrete vocabulary
compute_cost: approximately three times baseline inference cost
limitations: does not explicitly remove scenario-invalid actions
